topic: Two-zone disc topology — V13 cross-equatorial fit (post-c3-patch verify)
change_summary: c3 self-applied three patches (Hole-1 post-hoc rider rewrite, Hole-2 derives_from_dome flipped to false, Hole-3 population 40%→18-20% with tropical-band clarifier). Decider pushed queue_id=312 for c4 verify-patch. ISS-1879 (WGS84 defense preemption strengthening) and ISS-1880 (template-misapplication audit on WIN-056) remain open per c3 outputs.
our_argument_summary: WIN-057 (verdict: Misleading) attacks the V13 two-zone disc topology on three grounds: (1) the equatorial ring radius 14,105 km is a free parameter fitted to six known WGS84 city-pair distances, not derived from dome geometry; (2) NH RMSE regressed from 5.2% to 7.3% while cross-equatorial improved from 25–78% to 6.2%; (3) the 6.2% RMSE result remains ~600× worse than standard geodesy (Vincenty/WGS84 sub-0.01% with two parameters) and silently excludes near-equatorial cities below ~12° latitude (OPEN-015). Post-c3, the detail_verdict_text now contains a WIN-specific post-hoc rider followed by an explicit WGS84-symmetry preemption (the in-sample/out-of-sample asymmetry response), code_analysis.derives_from_dome=false, and the detail_extra population figure is corrected to 18–20% / ~1.5 billion with a tropical-band clarifier preempting the ±23.5°/40% quote-mine.

relevant_files: raw-text/02b-wins-V51.txt section on V13 topology (unchanged since c3)
raw-text/monitor.py (no monitoring entry for this WIN per code_analysis.monitoring='none')
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: V13 dome introduces two-zone topology with an additional fitted parameter (equatorial ring radius = 14,105 km) added on top of H₀, λ in H(r)=H₀·exp(−r/λ), and EW angular scale 0.9941. The fitting is to six cross-equatorial city-pair distances from WGS84 published values. The dome's monitor.py has no live-fetch domain or hardcoded comparison for this WIN; the validation is documented in static prose only.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: monitoring=none (correctly tagged). Static topology claim, not a per-domain prediction with tolerance band.
data_sources_used: Six known WGS84 city-pair distances chosen as fit targets
OPEN-015 published acknowledgment of equatorial exclusion zone
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: OPEN-015 explicitly excludes cities within ~12° of equator from the model's domain. The dome reports no out-of-sample validation (short routes, equator-to-NH routes, SH-internal routes beyond the showcase six).
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None remaining post-c3. The c3-flagged self-contradiction between detail_evidence ('not derived from dome geometry') and the prior generic post-hoc-template rider ('derives from its own geometric or symbolic parameters') is resolved — the rider has been rewritten to WIN-specific language and the code_analysis.derives_from_dome tag is now false, matching the evidence text and matching WIN-027 within the same detail_group.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: monitor.py has no domain entry for V13 two-zone topology; the claim is a static prose+table comparison, not a running prediction harness. Unchanged since c3.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Standard physics does not predict a two-zone equatorial-ring topology with a separate ring radius parameter; the dome's V13 topology has no globe-physics analog. Unchanged since c3.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Six known WGS84 city-pair distances are public, indexed, and were used as the fit targets (the 'observation' that the V13 'prediction' matches). The dome's V13 release post-dates the existence of those distances. Unchanged since c3.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: POST-c3-PATCH: tag flipped from true→false to match detail_evidence ('not derived from dome geometry'). The 14,105 km equatorial ring radius is fitted to six city-pair distances, not derived from dome parameters (disc_radius=20,015 km, firmament_height=9,086 km, sun_altitude=5,733 km, moon_altitude=2,534 km). The V13 topological framework is dome-stipulated, but per the strict tag definition in CLAUDE.md ('the predicted value is mathematically derived from the dome's geometric equations'), the predicted numerical value here is curve-fit, not derived. This now agrees with WIN-027 (false) within detail_group WIN-027/057/069.

holes_found.description: Cross-WIN inconsistency now points the other way: WIN-069 outlier. The c3 chose option (a) 'flip WIN-057 to false' over option (b) 'add WIN-069-style nuance and keep true'. That resolved the WIN-057 self-contradiction but shifted the within-detail_group inconsistency to: WIN-027=false, WIN-057=false, WIN-069=true (with derives_from_dome_evidence noting 'numerical parameters are fit to globe data'). WIN-069 is now structurally identical to WIN-057 (V13 framework dome-stipulated; numerical parameters fit) but tagged opposite. Per the strict CLAUDE.md tag definition — 'the predicted value is mathematically derived from the dome's geometric equations' — WIN-069's nuance is overgenerous: 'V13 topology is dome-derived' answers a different question than 'is the predicted value derived from dome geometry.' A defender can quote-mine the WIN-027/057/069 trio to argue the reviewers can't agree on what 'derives from dome' means within a single detail_group.
holes_found.recommendation: File ISS-NNNN against WIN-069 to flip derives_from_dome to false, matching WIN-027 and WIN-057. The strict tag definition is value-centric, not framework-centric; WIN-069's geometric-framework-vs-numerical-parameter distinction is descriptively true but doesn't override the tag's value-derivation criterion. Alternatively, if the framework-centric reading is preferred, all three should flip back to true with WIN-069's nuance added — but that re-opens the c3 self-contradiction in WIN-057's evidence text, so option (a) is cleaner.

holes_found.description: ISS-1879 partial coverage: WGS84-was-also-fit defense preemption is integrated (response 1 from c3 advocate_mode_preemptive_recommendation: in-sample/out-of-sample asymmetry). NOT integrated: response 2 (Clarke 1866 / Bessel 1841 early-geodetic-models comparison) and stronger_arguments item 2 (AIC/BIC information-criterion framing). The defender's strongest fair-minded counter — 'iterative refinement is normal science; early geodesy was 0.5% before WGS84' — has no rebuttal in the verdict text. The current verdict text addresses 'WGS84 was also fit' in absolute-accuracy terms (sub-cm vs 6.2%) and out-of-sample terms, but does not address the iterative-refinement framing where the dome could claim it's at the 'Clarke 1866' stage of its own development.
holes_found.recommendation: Augment detail_verdict_text with a one-sentence Clarke/Bessel comparison: 'Early geodetic models (Clarke 1866, Bessel 1841) reached <0.5% relative error on continental baselines; the dome's V13 reaches 6.2% on six chosen routes and excludes a substantial fraction of the surface — iterative refinement does not save a model that is regressing from first-principles architecture toward globe data.' Alternatively, add the AIC/BIC framing if academic precision is preferred. Both sentences would close ISS-1879 and end the iterative-refinement defense path. ISS-1879 remains open — c4 verifies the partial coverage but does not unblock further work.

holes_found.description: tldr_verdict says '...still 600× worse than standard geodesy's sub-meter accuracy.' Vincenty on WGS84 achieves sub-millimeter to sub-centimeter accuracy (10⁻⁵ to 10⁻⁷ of route length), not sub-meter. 'Sub-meter' is technically not false (sub-cm IS sub-meter) but it understates standard geodesy's accuracy by ~3 orders of magnitude. A casual reader sees 'sub-meter' and 'dome 6.2% RMSE on 6500 km route' (~400 km) and may think the gap is one order of magnitude when it is actually six. Per the TLDR severity guideline ('imprecision that doesn't mislead is minor at most'), this is borderline — the imprecision could mislead a reader who doesn't expand to the detail. The detail body correctly says 'sub-0.01%' and 'sub-centimeter'.
holes_found.recommendation: Change tldr_verdict tail from 'sub-meter accuracy' to 'sub-centimeter accuracy' or 'sub-millimeter accuracy' to match the verdict body. Suggested: 'The best result (6.2% RMSE) is still 600× worse than standard geodesy's sub-centimeter accuracy.' Single-word fix.

holes_found.description: The post-hoc rider phrase 'in-sample retrodiction, not prediction' is precise but jargony for the casual reader. The c3-recommended phrase landed verbatim, which is good — but a defender might pick at 'retrodiction' as a loaded term. The CLAUDE.md and SCIENTIFIC-CONTEXT references suggest the term is in scope for the review's framing, but a one-clause gloss ('i.e., a fit to existing data, not a forecast of new data') would harden it against the 'jargon as obfuscation' charge.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional: add a parenthetical gloss to the closing line. 'Under the dome author's own definition and our stricter standard in Part 3.1b, this is in-sample retrodiction (i.e., a fit to data already in hand), not prediction.' Low priority — the term is correctly used and any reader who clicks Part 3.1b will find the definition there.

stronger_arguments.description: The Australia-road-scaffold parallel: WIN-069 (same detail_group) shows the dome's V13 framework with refractive profile achieves matching results on AGD66→GDA94 by fitting numerical parameters to globe data. That is a second instance of the same 'fit to known data, then claim the framework predicts the data' move. A one-sentence cross-reference in WIN-057's detail_extra strengthens the verdict by showing the pattern is not a one-off: 'WIN-069 (Australia road scaffold) exhibits the same fit-to-globe-data pattern, suggesting V13 is being iteratively tuned to specific regional datasets rather than predicting them from dome geometry.'
sources: data/wins.json:WIN-069 detail_evidence (V13+refractive numerical parameter fit to AGD66→GDA94)

stronger_arguments.description: Out-of-sample validation challenge — concrete experimental proposal. Suggest one sentence in detail_verdict_text: 'A genuine out-of-sample test would compute V13 predictions for 100 randomly-chosen city pairs not used in the original fit and compare against WGS84-derived ground truth; the dome has not published this, and absent that publication the 6.2% RMSE figure has no inferential weight beyond the six routes used to fit it.' This converts the in-sample/out-of-sample critique from theoretical to operational — a defender now has to either produce the test or concede the asymmetry.

kernel_of_truth.description: The c3 patches strengthen the WIN's argumentation in three concrete ways visible in the current data/wins.json:WIN-057: (a) the rewritten post-hoc rider in detail_verdict_text (from this c4 review's reading of the live field) now matches the actual fit-vs-derive fact pattern instead of the generic template; (b) the code_analysis.derives_from_dome=false tag now agrees with the detail_evidence text 'not derived from dome geometry' and with WIN-027 in the same detail_group; (c) the detail_extra population claim '18–20% of world population (approximately 1.5 billion)' is defensible against gridded-population datasets and includes an explicit clarifier preempting the ±23.5°-tropical-band/40% quote-mine. The verdict (Misleading) and the underlying scientific argument were already correct in c2/c3; the c3 patches close the internal-consistency exploits a defender could have used.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The c3 patches resolve the internal contradictions but do not change the underlying scientific verdict, which c2/c3 already had correct (see data/wins.json:WIN-057 detail_evidence and detail_verdict_text). The kernel of 'patches landed cleanly' supports recommending no_change on the verdict and minor_edit on remaining items (cross-WIN consistency with WIN-069 per holes_found item 1; ISS-1879 strengthening per item 2; tldr 'sub-meter' precision per item 3). Misleading remains the right verdict; a c5 is not warranted unless the WIN-069 inconsistency or the AIC/BIC strengthening is acted on.

advocate_mode.best_defense: I am a fair-minded dome defender reading the post-c3 verdict text. The reviewers now explicitly concede that WGS84 was also fit to data, so they can't simply call my 14,105 km parameter 'curve-fitting.' Their distinction (in-sample-on-six-routes vs WGS84's billions of out-of-sample points) is the strongest fair argument they have, but it is a quantitative argument about coverage, not a structural argument about scientific method. I have two responses: (1) every empirical model starts somewhere — WGS84 was a 1984 update on Bessel 1841, Clarke 1866, ED50, NAD27, etc. The dome is at the 'Clarke 1866' stage of its empirical refinement; expecting V13 to match a 200-year-iteratively-refined ellipsoid is unfair. (2) The reviewers concede V13 reduces RMSE from 25–78% to 6.2% and frame this as 'real but expected' — but that concession is the entire claim. The dome model is iteratively improving against ground truth; the reviewers' 'still 600× worse' framing isn't a refutation, it's a statement about absolute accuracy at the current iteration. The reviewers should compare the trajectory of dome RMSE across versions (V12→V13) to the trajectory of geodetic RMSE across decades (Clarke→Bessel→…→WGS84), not the endpoint.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Add one sentence to detail_verdict_text addressing the trajectory framing: 'The iterative-refinement framing (V13 is at the Clarke 1866 stage) misses that early geodetic models started at <0.5% on continental baselines and converged to sub-cm globally without ever requiring an exclusion zone — the dome's V13 starts at 6.2% on six routes with a permanent ±12° exclusion zone, so the trajectory analogy implies a model fundamentally bounded by its parameter space, not a model in early development.' This closes the trajectory-defense path that the c3 advocate_mode (defense_survives=3) flagged but the c3 patch only partially addressed. With this addition, defense_survives drops from 2 to 1 (only AIC/BIC academic framing remains as a strengthening opportunity, and that is a 'we could be more rigorous' issue, not a 'defender survives' issue). ISS-1879 closure should be gated on this addition.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: WIN-069 outlier in detail_group WIN-027/057/069: WIN-027=false, WIN-057=false (post-c3-patch), WIN-069=true with derives_from_dome_evidence. Per holes_found item 1, file new ISS to flip WIN-069 to false. Separately, the generic post-hoc-template phrase 'derives from its own geometric or symbolic parameters' is still present in WIN-012, WIN-030, WIN-056, WIN-069 (verified via raw-string search of data/wins.json by this curmudgeon run). ISS-1880 covers WIN-056 specifically; WIN-012, WIN-030, WIN-069 are not yet covered. Recommend extending ISS-1880 (or filing companion issues) to audit all four remaining template-bearing WINs against their specific evidence — if the same template-misapplication bug repeats, the c3 audit is incomplete. The c3 cross_win_consistency section flagged WIN-056 but missed WIN-012, WIN-030, WIN-069 from the same template family.

claims_checked: 6.2% RMSE is ~600× worse than WGS84/Vincenty sub-0.01% (verdict body)
Dome uses 4+ fitted parameters vs WGS84's 2 (verdict body, detail_evidence)
18–20% of world population within ~12° of equator (detail_extra, post-c3 patch)
1.5 billion people within ~12° band (detail_extra, post-c3 patch)
quantitative_verification.computations: Ratio 6.2/0.01 = 620, '600×' verified within rounding. Counted parameters from detail_evidence: H₀, λ, EW angular scale 0.9941, equatorial ring radius 14,105 km — 4 explicit parameters, '4+' verified. WGS84: a=6,378,137 m, f=1/298.257 — 2 parameters, verified. Population: 18% of ~8 billion = ~1.44 billion; 20% of ~8 billion = ~1.6 billion; '1.5 billion' falls within range, verified. Tropical-band clarifier (±23.5° ≈ 40%) verified against gridded population product family (SEDAC GPW). TLDR cross-check: 'sub-meter' is technically true (sub-cm ⊂ sub-m) but verdict body says 'sub-0.01%' which on a 6500 km route is ~650 m — wait, 0.01% of 6500 km is 0.65 km = 650 m. So '600× worse' compares 6.2% to 0.01%. But Vincenty's actual practical accuracy is much better than 0.01% — typically sub-mm for the inverse formulae per Vincenty 1975. The 'sub-0.01%' figure is itself a conservative ceiling on Vincenty's residual; real-world WGS84 Vincenty residuals are in the millimeter range. So the verdict body's '600× worse' may itself slightly understate the gap (the real ratio could be 6.2%/<10⁻⁵ ≈ 6×10⁵, i.e., 600,000×), but the round-up to 600× is defensible as a conservative statement and is consistent with how the gap is communicated in standard geodesy textbooks.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: TLDR 'sub-meter' downgrades the standard-geodesy accuracy benchmark by ~3 orders of magnitude relative to the verdict body's 'sub-0.01%' / 'sub-centimeter'. Flagged as minor in holes_found item 3. No factual errors in the body.

deeper_analysis: The c3 patches landed cleanly. The big picture: c3 had a within-WIN self-contradiction (post-hoc rider said 'derives from dome geometry' while detail_evidence said 'not derived from dome geometry') plus an across-WIN inconsistency (WIN-027=false, WIN-057=true, WIN-069=true). Decider self-applied option (a) — flip WIN-057 to false and rewrite the rider to WIN-specific language. That resolved the within-WIN issue and aligned WIN-027/057, but pushed the across-WIN issue onto WIN-069. WIN-069 is now the outlier with derives_from_dome=true while WIN-027/057 in the same detail_group are false. Per the strict CLAUDE.md tag definition (predicted *value* derives from dome geometry), WIN-069's nuance is overgenerous and should also be false. This is the new c4 finding (holes_found item 1). ISS-1879 (WGS84 defense preemption strengthening) is partially addressed by the post-c3 verdict text — response (1) of the c3 advocate_mode_preemptive_recommendation is integrated (in-sample/out-of-sample asymmetry) but response (2) Clarke/Bessel iterative-refinement comparison and AIC/BIC framing are not. ISS-1879 remains open as a known item; this c4 reduces the c3 advocate_mode defense_survives from 3 to 2, with one further strengthening sentence (the trajectory framing in advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation above) capable of dropping it to 1. The TLDR 'sub-meter' undersells standard geodesy by ~3 orders of magnitude vs the verdict body's 'sub-0.01%' / 'sub-centimeter'; this is a single-word fix worth doing. Cross-template audit: the generic post-hoc-template phrase 'derives from its own geometric or symbolic parameters' is still present in WIN-012, WIN-030, WIN-056, WIN-069. ISS-1880 covers WIN-056; WIN-012/030/069 are not yet covered. Recommend extending the audit. Fairness check: I am marking current_verdict_holds=true and recommended_action=minor_edit. The patches are real and valuable; the verdict (Misleading) was always correct; the c4 findings are about strengthening the framing further, not about errors. A defender reading this WIN post-c3 has materially less ammunition than they had post-c2.

citations_verified: Vincenty 1975 doi:10.1179/sre.1975.23.176.88 (Survey Review) — DOI shape valid; canonical sub-mm WGS84 reference. Unchanged since c3.
OPEN-015 dome-side reference — dome author's own published open problem confirming <12° exclusion. Unchanged since c3.
Internal anchor #posthoc-standard — 1 occurrence in data/sections.json (Part 3.1b), anchor exists. Unchanged since c3.
citations_unchecked: https://john09289.github.io/predictions (dome author's site definition page) — content reachable; existence of 'dome author's own definition' under that URL not byte-checked this run. Same as c3 — flagged for poller's periodic check.

summary_for_decider: c4 verify-patch: all three c3 patches landed cleanly. Verdict (Misleading) holds. Three new findings: (1) moderate — cross-WIN inconsistency now points at WIN-069 (was WIN-057); recommend filing new ISS to flip WIN-069 to derives_from_dome=false matching strict tag definition. (2) moderate — ISS-1879 partially addressed; trajectory/Clarke-Bessel rebuttal missing. Recommend single sentence add. (3) minor — tldr_verdict 'sub-meter' undersells. Recommend single-word change to 'sub-centimeter'. ALSO: extend ISS-1880's audit scope to WIN-012/WIN-030/WIN-069 — same generic post-hoc template phrase still present, c3 audit was incomplete.
